qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Prototype poisoning in plain-object key-path helpers injects inherited properties
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding depends on Immutable.setIn()/updateIn() mutating plain-object prototypes through updateIn/set/shallowCopy, while the advisory describes unguarded __proto__ handling in merge()/mergeDeep()/mergeDeepWith(), Map.toJS(), and Map.toObject().

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The session's only qualifying finding is a separate prototype-poisoning bug in setIn()/updateIn() on plain JavaScript objects, not the advisory's merge/toJS/toObject root cause.
evidence: The advisory details and referenced patch guard __proto__ in src/functional/merge.js, src/toJS.*, and src/methods/toObject.js for mergeDeep/merge/mergeDeepWith and Map.toJS()/Map.toObject().
The qualifying finding's report and PoC are tied to src/functional/updateIn.ts, src/functional/set.ts, and src/utils/shallowCopy.ts, with proof only for Immutable.setIn() and Immutable.updateIn().
The finding does not show attacker-controlled data reaching the advisory's merge or plain-object materialization code paths, so the vulnerable code and control flow do not align.
